title: Authentication, Sessions, Passwords, Bearer Tokens, and Credential Recovery · Execution Trace: Password Storage Login Defense
summary: A service invents password hashing, trusts JWT payloads before verification, accepts any algorithm and audience, stores long-lived bearer tokens in logs, and leaves sessions valid after recovery. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo password storage login defense increment in authentication and credential lifecycle policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use reviewed password hashing, bounded comparison, generic failures, rate and abuse controls, MFA or step-up policy, and secure recovery lifecycle.
Separate presented credential, verification, authenticated principal, session, assurance, freshness, and downstream authorization.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
